channel.title: parity on Noam Zaks
channel.description: Recent content in parity on Noam Zaks

item.title: Square Covering
item.description: An odd number of $ n \times n $ squares is placed on an infinite grid, such that any two may overlap. Show that there are at least $ n^2 $ small $ 1 \times 1 $ grid squares that are covered by an odd number of squares. First, let’s create an animations showing this for $ n = 3 $: The code looks like so: from manimlib.imports import * class Example(Scene): def construct(self): # Create 3x3 squares one = Square(fill_opacity=0.

item.title: Square Covering Variations
item.description: This page contains variations of the square covering parity riddle. You should read it before continuing. Rectangles There are a few things that the proof did not require (hence we can generalize it). For example, we never really used the fact that we are describing square. The riddle can be easily described for rectangles: An odd number of $ m \times n $ rectangles is placed on an infinite grid, such that any two may overlap.
